summary: Verified /industries-served hub + 14 child industry pages render correctly with brief shell, sections, back link, and 404-redirect behavior. All 5 refactored discipline pages (operational-discipline, frontline-leadership, equipment-reliability, workforce-capability, daily-accountability) render the new Brief shell (BriefHeader + 'What We Build' eyebrow + correct H1 + BriefFooter, no legacy navy iframe). Global Results > What We Build dropdown routes correctly. BUT discovered a CRITICAL clip-path bug: the final CTA <section> on both the hub (hub-cta-contact, hub-cta-cases) and every industry page (industry-cta-contact-<slug>, industry-cta-cases-<slug>) is invisible and unclickable in production because that <section> is rendered as a raw element without the useInViewClass IntersectionObserver hook, so .is-in is never added and the .wipe children stay permanently clipped (clip-path: inset(-6.4px 100% -8px 0px)).

ui_bugs.issue: Raw <section className='brief-doc-station brief-doc-cta'> is NOT wrapped with the <Section> helper or useInViewClass ref, so .is-in class never gets applied. As a result every .wipe descendant (station-index 'Don't See Your Industry?', H2 'Find your industry above...', lede paragraph, and the wipe-d3 div containing BOTH hub-cta-contact and hub-cta-cases buttons) keeps the initial clip-path: inset(-0.4em 100% -0.5em 0) and renders as a blank navy band. Buttons are invisible to users and unclickable (elementFromPoint at button center returns brief-doc-inner, not the <a>). Screenshot at 1440x900 after scrolling to bottom shows an empty navy block above the footer.

Same root cause as hub: raw <section className='brief-doc-station brief-doc-cta'> on every industry-served/:slug page lacks useInViewClass. The 'When You're Ready' eyebrow, H2, lede, and BOTH industry-cta-contact-<slug> + industry-cta-cases-<slug> buttons are permanently clipped and unclickable on all 14 industry pages. Confirmed on /industries-served/agribusiness — click times out and elementFromPoint at button center returns brief-doc-inner.

integration_issues.issue: hub-cta-contact and hub-cta-cases never receive pointer events due to the clip-path bug above; force-click also fails to navigate (button is rendered with 0 visible area).
Same — 28 affected buttons (14 contact + 14 cases) across all industry pages.

action_items: Wrap the final CTA <section> in /app/frontend/src/pages/IndustriesServed.jsx (line ~97) using the existing <Section> helper at the top of the file, OR attach a ref via useRef + useInViewClass(ref) directly to that <section>. Same fix needed in /app/frontend/src/components/IndustryPage.jsx (line ~134). Either approach will let the IntersectionObserver add .is-in, which uncovers the clip-path and restores both visibility and click hit-area on all CTA buttons.
After fixing, re-run the click test on hub-cta-contact/hub-cta-cases and at least one industry-cta-contact-<slug>/industry-cta-cases-<slug> to confirm navigation works.
critical_code_review_comments: Pattern bug: every .brief-doc-station section MUST use the <Section> helper (or otherwise attach useInViewClass) otherwise its .wipe descendants stay clipped. The two raw <section> tags found here violate the pattern. Worth grepping the rest of the brief-doc pages for the same anti-pattern (raw <section className="brief-doc-station ..."> without ref). A defensive enhancement would be to make BriefDocStyles fall back to a non-clipped state if .is-in never lands within N ms (e.g. via a default rule and an animation-completion guard), so a missing IntersectionObserver doesn't silently hide content.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (hub + 14 industry pages + 5 discipline pages all render correctly; only the final-CTA band on 15 pages is broken)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration_14 verified at 1440x900: (a) /industries-served hub H1 'Different industries. The same execution discipline.' renders, all 5 group labels present, all 14 industry-card-<slug> data-testids present. (b) All 14 /industries-served/:slug pages return HTTP 200, render H1 + back link industry-back-<slug> + all 5 canonical sections (The Pressures, How We Work in <Name>, The Outcomes, Sub-segments, Why POWERS for <Name>) + final CTA buttons. (c) Back link click navigates to /industries-served. (d) /industries-served/does-not-exist correctly redirects to /industries-served (Navigate replace). (e) All 5 discipline pages render brief shell with correct H1 and 'What We Build' eyebrow, 0 iframes. (f) Global header Results > What We Build dropdown shows all 5 disciplines and clicking 'Operational Discipline' navigates to /operational-discipline. (g) No /industries-served/:slug child links appear in global header nav. (h) Zero console errors observed. ONLY OUTSTANDING ISSUE = clip-path bug on the two raw <section> final-CTA blocks described above. After main agent fixes those two files, retest clicks on the four data-testids: hub-cta-contact, hub-cta-cases, industry-cta-contact-agribusiness, industry-cta-cases-agribusiness.
rca of the issue: Root cause: BriefDocStyles.css line 333-338 defines .wipe with clip-path: inset(-0.4em 100% -0.5em 0) revealed only when ancestor .brief-doc-station has .is-in. The .is-in class is added by the useInViewClass(ref) hook (BriefDocStyles.jsx ~line 426) which is wired up inside the <Section> helper component used by every other content section. The final 'When You're Ready' CTA section in both IndustriesServed.jsx (line 97) and IndustryPage.jsx (line 134) was authored as a raw <section> tag (likely because it needed a custom centered style) WITHOUT the useInViewClass hook, so its descendants' clip-path never transitions to inset(... 0 ... 0). Result: content is laid out (button has bounding rect 262x56.5) but paint is clipped to 0-width and pointer events flow through to the parent .brief-doc-inner. Fix is one of: (1) wrap with existing <Section> helper, (2) add const ctaRef = useRef(null); useInViewClass(ctaRef); and put ref={ctaRef} on the section, or (3) add a default reveal rule like `@media (prefers-reduced-motion) .brief-doc-station .wipe { clip-path: none; }` plus a JS fallback timeout.
